raw_output_payload.reasoning: We need a bash command to copy all files in /system except those containing 'text' to destination. Use find with -not -name '*text*' and -type f -exec cp --parents {} /system/folder3/backup_dbg/backup \; or use rsync --exclude='*text*'. Simpler: rsync -av --exclude='*text*' /system/ /system/folder3/backup_dbg/backup/. That copies all files and directories. But requirement: copy all files in /system directory except those containing 'text'. Probably only files, not directories. Use find. Command: find /system -type f ! -name '*text*' -exec cp {} /system/folder3/backup_dbg/backup/ \; . Ensure destination exists. Provide single command.
raw_output_payload.reasoning_content: We need a bash command to copy all files in /system except those containing 'text' to destination. Use find with -not -name '*text*' and -type f -exec cp --parents {} /system/folder3/backup_dbg/backup \; or use rsync --exclude='*text*'. Simpler: rsync -av --exclude='*text*' /system/ /system/folder3/backup_dbg/backup/. That copies all files and directories. But requirement: copy all files in /system directory except those containing 'text'. Probably only files, not directories. Use find. Command: find /system -type f ! -name '*text*' -exec cp {} /system/folder3/backup_dbg/backup/ \; . Ensure destination exists. Provide single command.
